content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

July 19, 1990

Roxanne Heacock  
Office Manager  
San Diego County Deputy Sheriffs' Assn.  
7546 Trade Street  
San Diego, CA 92121

Re: Your Request for Advice  
Our File No. A-90-421

Dear Ms. Heacock:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided to you concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act.1

The following advice is based upon your letter, dated June 11, 1990, and a subsequent telephone conversation with you on June 28, 1990.

You have asked about use by the San Diego County Deputy Sheriffs' Association (DSA) of a "stamp" indicating their endorsement of a candidate. The stamp would be used by your office on all outgoing mail during the normal course of business. We advised that if the DSA's staff uses the stamp on regular outgoing mail, an employee would probably spend less than 10% of his or her time rendering this service. The cost would be de minimis and, therefore, not considered a contribution. (Regulation 18423.)

However, if you instruct your contract promoters to use the stamp on outgoing mail, or if you instruct your contract promoters to end telephone solicitations with a comment stating that the association is endorsing a certain candidate, you have made a non-monetary contribution. The amount of the in-kind contribution is a pro-rata cost of the mailing or phone solicitation. (Olson Advice Letter, No. A-87-318, copy enclosed.)

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

A copy of your letter is enclosed for your convenience.

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Sandra L. Taylor
Political Reform Consultant

cc: Linda Bunch
Registrar of Voters, San Diego County
